mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days exclusively during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the predictive power reverses to negative during early-cycle recoveries.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained job creation and rising household income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns across all market conditions, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicate persistent supply-side constraints and weak business investment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during post-shock recoveries fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal loses statistical significance when tested against a null model.
